mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and stable consumer price growth indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL move together, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals improving manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings capacity, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 240.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or the threshold signal loses predictive significance during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts demonstrates synchronized labor market strength and residential investment demand, which boosts consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market tightness and rising wage pressures, which compresses corporate margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle expansion, or Rank IC exceeds 0.02 indicating positive returns in this regime.
